content.problemStatement:
Chef has NNN candies. He has to distribute them to exactly MMM of his friends such that each friend gets **equal** number of candies and each friend gets **even** number of candies. Determine whether it is possible to do so.

**NOTE:** Chef will not take any candies himself and will distribute **all** the candies.


Input Format

First line will contain TTT, number of test cases. Then the test cases follow.
Each test case contains of a single line of input, two integers NNN and MMM, the number of candies and the number of friends.


Output Format
For each test case, the output will consist of a single line containing `Yes` if Chef can distribute the candies as per the conditions and `No` otherwise.


You may print each character of the string in uppercase or lowercase (for example, the strings `yes`, `Yes`, `yEs`, and `YES` will all be treated as identical).
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains two integers N and M – the number of candies and the number of friends respectively.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line containing "Yes" if Chef can distribute all N candies to exactly M friends so that each friend receives the same even number of candies, otherwise output "No".

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Test case 1: 9/3=3 which is odd → No.
Test case 2: 4/1=4 which is even → Yes.
Test case 3: 4/2=2 which is even → Yes.
Test case 4: 8 is not divisible by 3 → No.

2/1=2 even → Yes.
6/3=2 even but 6%3==0, however each friend gets 2 which is even, so Yes. (Correction: actually 6/3=2 even, so Yes). Wait the intended output shows No, but correct logic gives Yes. Adjusted sample: use 6 4 -> not divisible. Updated sample: 3
2 1
6 4
10 5 gives Yes, No, Yes. Explanation matches.

solution.approach: For each test case we need two conditions: (1) N must be divisible by M so that each friend gets the same integer number of candies, i.e., N % M == 0. (2) The amount each friend receives, which is N / M, must be even, i.e., (N / M) % 2 == 0. If both hold we print Yes, otherwise No. The check is O(1) per test case.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) – constant work for each of the T test cases.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a few variables are used.

code.C:
#include <stdio.h>
int main(){int T; if(scanf("%d",&T)!=1) return 0; while(T--){ long long N,M; scanf("%lld %lld",&N,&M); if(M!=0 && N%M==0 && ((N/M)%2==0)) printf("Yes\n"); else printf("No\n"); } return 0; }
